response:
The prefix given to the end of the name to avoid confusion for the winner of the 1996 Record of the Year Grammy is **"Kiss"**.

This is derived from the song **"Kiss from a Rose"**, which was the winner of the **Record of the Year** Grammy in 1996. The song's title, **"Kiss from a Rose"**, was used to avoid confusion with the album **"Seal"** (the second eponymous album by Seal), which was also titled **"Seal"**. The title **"Kiss from a Rose"** was chosen to distinguish the song from the album, thus avoiding confusion.
